commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a63ecac9989ea9951a04a79aed96cb9c473d55b7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the assistant-message gate from `writeBranchedSession` and adds a regression test for writing a forked transcript when the parent branch has only a user turn.

PR surface: Source -9, Tests +55. Total +46 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: a parent transcript with a header plus user message and no assistant hits the current `writeBranchedSession` no-write gate, and the PR branch still needs full child-run coverage because preparation clears that same no-assistant shape. I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor or maintainer repair plus better proof; the blocker crosses the fork writer and runner preparation boundary rather than a one-line automation-safe cleanup.

target.title: fix(agents): persist forked transcript when parent branch has no assistant turn
target.body:
## Summary

A subagent spawned with `context="fork"` (and the realtime voice-consult fork and the reply-session fork) could silently start with an **empty** transcript, dropping the parent conversation it was meant to inherit, with no error surfaced.

**Root cause** (`src/auto-reply/reply/session-fork.runtime.ts`): `forkSessionFromParentRuntime` (:285) picks `writeBranchedSession` when the parent transcript has entries (`source.leafId` is non-null) and `writeForkHeaderOnly` when it does not. `writeForkHeaderOnly` always writes the new file. `writeBranchedSession` (:243) computed the forked entries but wrote the file **only inside `if (hasAssistant)`** (:270-281), while returning `{ sessionId, sessionFile }` **unconditionally** (:282).

So when the parent's leaf branch is a persisted user turn with **no assistant message** in it, the fork returns a `sessionFile` path that was never created. Callers then register the child with that path and `forkedFromParent: true`:

- `src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts:515` stores `sessionId` + `sessionFile` + `forkedFromParent: true` for `context="fork"`.
- `src/talk/agent-consult-runtime.ts:156` (realtime voice consult).
- `src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts:744` (reply-session fork).

When the child later opens that path, the file is absent, so it begins a fresh empty session: the inherited context is lost silently (the fork result is non-null, so the `if (!fork)` guard at `subagent-spawn.ts:505` never trips).

This is a real on-disk state, not a hand-crafted one: the gateway transcript writer (`appendSessionTranscriptMessage` in `src/config/sessions/transcript.ts`) persists a user message immediately (header + user entry) before the assistant reply exists, so a session whose opening user turn was persisted before/without an assistant reply (in-flight, or an assistant attempt that ended before persisting) is exactly `[session header, user message]`. `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.test.ts:311` already exercises this "session file has only user message (no assistant flush)" state.

**Fix:** `writeBranchedSession` now writes the branched transcript unconditionally, matching `writeForkHeaderOnly`. The invariant both fork-write paths share is restored: the returned `sessionFile` always exists. The forked child therefore inherits the parent branch (including a pending user turn) instead of starting blank.

**Why this is not one-sided** (sibling surfaces): the sibling write path `writeForkHeaderOnly` (the `leafId === null` case) already writes unconditionally, so this only aligns the divergent branch. All three callers consume the returned `sessionFile` identically (store it; the child opens it later); none relied on the file being absent as a signal, so no caller needs a companion change. The `hasAssistant` check had no other use and is removed (net prod LOC decreases).

## Diff size

```
 src/auto-reply/reply/session-fork.runtime.ts      | 23 +++++-------
 src/auto-reply/reply/session-fork.runtime.test.ts | 45 +++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 54 insertions(+), 15 deletions(-)
```

## Verification

Local (Node 22.19.0):

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session-fork.runtime.test.ts` — 7/7 pass (includes the new `persists the forked transcript when the parent branch has no assistant turn` regression).
- Caller suites green: `src/agents/subagent-spawn.context.test.ts` (7), `src/talk/agent-consult-runtime.test.ts` (8).
- `pnpm tsgo:core` — exit 0. `oxfmt --check` and `scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on both files — clean.
- Regression red-proof: reverting only the fix (restoring the `if (hasAssistant)` gate) makes the new test fail because `fork.sessionFile` does not exist; with the fix it exists and carries the parent's user message.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** a forked child session (subagent `context="fork"`, voice consult, or reply-session fork) whose parent's leaf branch has no assistant turn no longer silently starts empty; the returned fork `sessionFile` is always written and carries the parent branch.
- **Real environment tested:** the real production `forkSessionFromParentRuntime` from this checkout (the actual OpenClaw fork code path; the fork is invoked internally, there is no separate CLI verb), driven against a parent transcript in the on-disk shape the gateway writer produces (`[session header, user message]`), then reading the actual file it returns.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** wrote a parent transcript `{type:"session",...}` + `{type:"message",role:"user",content:"PENDING_PARENT_QUESTION"}`, called `forkSessionFromParentRuntime({ parentEntry, agentId:"main", sessionsDir })`, then checked `existsSync(fork.sessionFile)` and read the returned file's bytes.
- **Evidence after fix:** copied live console output from invoking the real `forkSessionFromParentRuntime` against that parent, before the change (on `origin/main`, the `hasAssistant` gate present) and after the change (this branch):

```
# origin/main  (hasAssistant gate present)
===FORK_SESSION_FILE=== /tmp/oc-fork-noassist-b2nYzd/sessions/2026-06-02T07-05-23-663Z_c6bd0dce-1cda-4686-9f51-214f5b95d80d.jsonl
===FORK_FILE_EXISTS=== false

# this branch  (fix applied)
===FORK_FILE_EXISTS=== true
===FORK_CARRIES_PARENT_MSG=== true
```

- **Observed result after fix:** the returned fork `sessionFile` exists on disk and its transcript contains the parent's pending user message `PENDING_PARENT_QUESTION`; on `origin/main` the identical input left `FORK_FILE_EXISTS=false`, so the child opened a non-existent transcript and lost the parent context.
- **What was not tested:** a fully live gateway turn that spawns a `context="fork"` subagent against an in-flight parent (requires a running Gateway plus model credentials, not available in this environment). The before/after above was exercised through the real production fork function and the actual file it returns.
target.author: yetval

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
